sources.name: Plain Language Act 2010
sources.note: Audience-first principle — identify who is reading before writing

sources.name: NARA 10 Principles of Plain Language
sources.note: Principle 1: Write for your audience. Establish audience before all else

_description: The first file the pipeline reads after pipeline.json. Defines the three facts the LLM must establish before generating a single word. If any cannot be determined, defaults.json provides the fallback value.
_rule: No output is generated until all three entry facts are resolved.

entry_facts.name: narrative
entry_facts.question: What is the narrative?
entry_facts.definition: The intent and facts the user has provided. This is the input. The pipeline does not invent, add to, or modify the narrative. It only controls how the narrative is expressed in language.
entry_facts.if_missing: Ask the user to clarify their intent before proceeding
entry_facts.what_pipeline_does_with_it: Uses narrative as the sole source of meaning. All vocabulary, sentence, and paragraph decisions serve the narrative — they do not shape it.

entry_facts.name: domain
entry_facts.question: What is the domain?
entry_facts.definition: The category of output being produced. Unlocks domain_register.json to set FK target and register ceiling.
entry_facts.resolution: Detect from narrative content and context signals
detection_signals: keywords indicating travel, risk, security → travel_risk
keywords indicating medical, patient, health, diagnosis → medical_patient
keywords indicating contract, liability, clause, legal → legal_contract
keywords indicating article, report, news, press → news_journalism
keywords indicating research, study, methodology, hypothesis → academic_research
keywords indicating emergency, evacuation, alert, urgent → crisis_operational
keywords indicating technical, specification, system, engineering → technical_specialist
keywords indicating report, assessment, briefing, summary → professional_report
entry_facts.what_pipeline_does_with_it: Passes domain to domain_register.json at step 3 to retrieve FK target and register ceiling

entry_facts.name: audience
entry_facts.question: What is the audience?
entry_facts.definition: Who will read the output. Sets the FK target ceiling and informs register ceiling selection.
entry_facts.resolution: Infer from domain or detect from explicit user instruction

audience_types.description: Mixed reading levels. No domain knowledge assumed. Default for most output.
Domain-adjacent readers. Some technical vocabulary permitted.
Expert readers. Full technical register permitted where domain tag matches.

entry_facts.what_pipeline_does_with_it: Confirms or adjusts the FK target set by domain_register.json

resolution_order: 2. Extract narrative — what is being said
3. Detect domain — what kind of document is this
4. Infer audience — who is reading it
5. If any fact is missing, apply defaults.json fallback
6. Pass all three facts to pipeline step 3 (domain_register.json)
hard_rule: The pipeline does not generate a single word until all three facts are resolved or defaulted.
